{{External|rs|rsc=Vampire slayer}}
{{Has quick guide|speedrun=1}}
{{Infobox Quest
|name = Vampyre Slayer
|number = 8
|image = [[File:Vampyre Slayer.png|300px]]
|release = [[28 January]] [[2001]]
|update = New Quest: Vampire Slayer
|members = No
|series = None
|developer = [[Paul Gower]]
}}
'''Vampyre Slayer''' is a [[free-to-play]] [[quest]] that takes place in [[Draynor Village]]. The player is asked to slay the terrifying [[vampyre]] count [[Count Draynor|Draynor]] terrorising the village that lives in a large mansion just north of Draynor, with the help of an experienced vampyre slayer. 

==Details==
{{Quest details
|start = Speak to [[Morgan]] in his house in [[Draynor Village]], just north of [[Ned]]'s house.
|startmap = 3098,3268
|difficulty = Intermediate
|description = The people of [[Draynor Village]] live in constant terror. Their numbers are dwindling, all due to the foul creature lurking in the manor to the north known as a vampyre.
|length = Very Short
|requirements = Able to defeat a [[combat level]] 34 [[Count Draynor|vampyre]] with a very high health recovery rate.
|items = *[[Hammer]] (can be bought from the [[general store]] just north of the [[Blue Moon Inn]] for 1 [[coin]])
* [[Beer]] (obtainable during the quest for 2 [[coins]])
* [[Stake]] (obtained during the quest)
|recommended = * {{SCP|Combat|20|link=yes}}
* [[Garlic]], highly recommended to weaken the Count (obtainable during the quest)
*Combat equipment - [[Magic]] is recommended if using the safespot
* Fast travel to [[Varrock]] (such as the [[Varrock teleport]])
* ''(Members only)'' Fast travel to [[Draynor Village]] ([[amulet of glory]] or [[Draynor Manor Teleport]])
|kills = [[Count Draynor]] ''(level 34)'' - safespottable
|leagueRegion = <poem>
Unlocking {{LeagueRegion|Morytania}} {{okay}} will auto-complete the quest
</poem>
}}

==Walkthrough==
===The vampyre threat===
{{Map|name=Morgan|x=3099|y=3269|r=3|mtype=square|align=right|width=200|height=200|caption=Morgan's location.}}
[[File:Morgan chathead.png|left|frameless]]
Speak to [[Morgan]] in his house in [[Draynor Village]], just north of [[Ned]]'s house. He will tell you that the village is being ravaged by an evil [[vampyre]] that lives in the basement of the nearby [[Draynor Manor|manor]], and they need someone to get rid of it. He wants you to first talk to the retired vampyre hunter [[Dr Harlow]] for tips on how to kill it.

If you don't already have [[garlic]], you can search the [[cupboard (Morgan's home)|cupboard]] upstairs to find some.


===The retired vampyre slayer===
[[File:Dr Harlow chathead.png|left|frameless]]
{{Map|name=Dr Harlow|x=3222|y=3397|r=4|mtype=square|align=right|width=200|height=200|caption=Dr Harlow's location at The Blue Moon Inn.}}
[[Dr Harlow]] can be found in the [[Blue Moon Inn]] in [[Varrock]], south of the square. Tell him [[Morgan]] needs his help, and he will ask for a [[beer]] in return, which you can buy from the bartender for 2 [[coins]]  if you didn't bring one. Talk to him again and he will give you a [[stake]], advise you to use a [[hammer]] to drive it in, and to bring some [[garlic]] to weaken the vampyre.

If you don't already have a [[hammer]], you can buy one from the [[Varrock General Store]] to the north for 1 [[coin]].

===Count Draynor===
{{Needed|[[weapon]],  [[armour]], [[food]], [[garlic]], [[hammer]] and a [[stake]]}}
[[File:Count Draynor.png|left|80px|frameless]]
[[File:Count draynor safespot.png|thumb|right|200px|A player fights against Count Draynor using the safespot.]]
Head to [[Draynor Manor]] north of [[Draynor Village]], bringing your combat equipment and [[food]], [[garlic]], a [[hammer]] and the [[stake]]. 

Once inside, go down the large stairs on the east side of the building. There are candles in this room that you can walk on, causing the player to exclaim, but these do not deal damage. There is a [[coffin (Vampyre Slayer)|coffin]] in the centre of the room. Open it and the vampyre, [[Count Draynor]], will pop up and attack you.

If you brought [[garlic]], upon beginning melee combat, the Count will automatically take 10  damage at the start of the fight, and have his attack/strength reduced by 10 levels, and defence by 40. This effect lasts for 30 seconds.

It is possible to [[safespot]] him by running to the south-western corner between the staircase and the skeleton. Once the Count is next to you, step into the tile he is occupying. He should move into the tile you just left, but this may take a couple of tries. Once he is in the corner, move to the square diagonal to him so that you are standing in front of the skeleton. If using the safespot, it is recommended to use [[Magic]] as [[flinching]] with melee is slow and the Count regenerates health at roughly 6 times the rate of regular monsters.

Upon defeating [[Count Draynor]], you will automatically stab him with the hammer and stake; if you didn't bring a hammer, {{Mes|You are unable to push the stake far enough in}} will appear in the chat, and you will be unable to kill him.

'''Note''': It is possible to set up a Dwarf Multi Cannon in this area.

'''Note''': If you take too long to kill Count Draynor, he returns to his coffin and heals to full health.

The quest is completed after defeating the count, and you can exit [[Draynor Manor]] through the exit in the eastern-most room or teleport.

'''Congratulations, quest complete!'''

==Reward==
{{Quest rewards
|name = Vampyre Slayer
|image = [[File:Vampyre Slayer reward scroll.png|centre]]
|qp = 3
|rewards =
*{{SCP|Attack|4,825}} [[Attack]] [[experience]]

Unlocks:
* [[Count Draynor]] accessible in the [[Nightmare Zone]].
}}

==Required for completing==
Completion of Vampyre Slayer is required for the following:
*[[Sins of the Father]]

==Transcript==
{{Hastranscript|quest}}

==Changes==
{{Subject changes header}}
{{Subject changes
|date = 4 June 2020
|update = Darkmeyer
|poll =
|change = The word "vampire" within the quest's name and dialogue was changed to "vampyre" for lore consistency.
}}
{{Subject changes footer}}

==Trivia==
*Players who complete this quest with an [[Attack]] level of 1 will gain 19 Attack levels, resulting in an Attack level of 20.
*This counts towards your slayer task if tasked with vampyres 

{{Vampyre Slayer}}
